title: Provider's Role: Importance of Vaccine Administration and Vaccine Storage & Handling | Vaccines & Immunizations | CDC
text:

Other materials can assist you in communicating with parents or patients about vaccine benefits and risks. Parents can also be referred to credible Internet and hotline resources, such as CDC-INFO at 1-800-CDC-INFO or 1-800-232-4636 (English and Español).
Be prepared to manage vaccine side effects 
Most people experience no side effects, or only mild ones, following immunization. Mild side effects may include soreness, swelling, or redness at the injection site or mild fever. Severe side effects, such as severe allergic reactions, following vaccination are extremely rare. However, any provider who administers vaccines should have procedures in place for the emergency care of a person who experiences an anaphylactic reaction. Epinephrine and equipment for maintaining an airway should be available for immediate use. All vaccine providers should be familiar with the office emergency plan and should be certified in cardiopulmonary resuscitation.
Report suspected side effects to VAERS 
The Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System (VAERS) is a national vaccine safety monitoring program. VAERS
